name: Satori Canton
title: AI Consultant · Board Member · Founder
tagline: I've caught two technology waves from the very beginning. Here's what I've learned.
bio:
Some people discover new technology. I've made a habit of being there when it's born.

In 1996, after a company layoff, I rallied a team of developers and founded Satori Interactive — a game studio that shipped titles published by Activision and Viacom through the late 90s. When Flash emerged and made the web genuinely interactive, we pivoted. I joined ActionScript.com as a staff writer, eventually bought the site outright, and rebranded the company as ActionScript.com in 2003. For the next decade it was the definitive hub for Flash developers worldwide. I was the lead ActionScript architect for FarmVille at Zynga, built the first Flash apps at RockYou (one of the largest social media platforms of the Web 2.0 era), and created experiences for Justin.tv (now Twitch), Bed Bath & Beyond, Frontier Airlines, and the Denver Broncos. When Flash was the internet, I owned it.

I didn't just show up for the AI wave in 2023. I'd been quietly researching neural networks and deep learning since 2012 — writing about AI and ML on Medium since 2018, publishing tutorials on Brain.js and deep-dives on every major art movement through an AI lens. When generative AI tools made these capabilities accessible to artists, I had a decade of context most of the community didn't. I founded StyleGuideAI, built a community of 1,000+ AI artists, and went deep on Flux, LoRA training, and generative model architecture. Today I serve as Head of Artificial Intelligence and Board Member at HeartStamp, where I rebuilt the company's entire generative AI strategy from scratch, led the AI engineering team, and guided a 30-person startup to its MVP launch in under six months.

What I bring is unusual: genuine technical depth in how these models work (I've trained them, researched them, and written about them for years), an artist's eye for what good output looks like, a product instinct for what matters to users, and a pattern-recognition muscle built from two technology waves. I sit comfortably between CEO vision and engineering execution. I'm transparent about what I don't know and direct about what I do.
summary: Satori Canton is an AI consultant and strategist with deep expertise in generative AI, AI agent architecture, and LLM integration — and an unusual 30-year track record of being an early adopter and community builder at major technology inflection points. He founded Satori Interactive in 1996 — a game studio that shipped titles published by Activision and Viacom — then pivoted to Flash development, acquired ActionScript.com, and rebranded the company as ActionScript.com in 2003, spending nearly two decades as one of the most recognized names in Flash/interactive web development (lead ActionScript architect for FarmVille at Zynga, first Flash developer at RockYou, early work with Justin.tv which became Twitch). He has been personally researching neural networks and deep learning since 2012, with hands-on Python experience in PyTorch and TensorFlow, and has published on his Medium blog (medium.com/@satoricanton) since 2018 — including practical tutorials on Brain.js (building and deploying neural networks on the web) and over 300 articles on art movements and how to achieve those styles with AI. He entered the generative AI space in 2023, founding StyleGuideAI (an AI consultancy and 1,000+ member AI artist community on Discord and DeviantArt), and is currently Head of Artificial Intelligence and Board Member at HeartStamp — an AI-powered greeting card startup — where he rebuilt the company's entire generative AI strategy from scratch, led LoRA model training and fine-tuning for Flux image models, engineers art prompts for frontier image models (NanoBanana 2 and ChatGPT-Image-1.5), authors system prompts for HeartStamp's production AI agent integrating the latest text-based LLM APIs, builds automated prompt regression test suites and golden datasets using LangSmith (LangChain) to ensure output quality across model updates, benchmarks models for latency/cost trade-offs, maintains prompt versioning in Git, architected AI agent workflows and RAG data systems, led the AI engineering team, and guided the company to its MVP launch. His consulting practice specializes in: generative image AI strategy (Flux, Stable Diffusion, LoRA training/fine-tuning), AI art direction and visual style consulting (art-historical style vocabulary, systematic prompt engineering for visual identity, aesthetic consistency across model outputs), AI agent and workflow design, prompt engineering and meta-prompting, system prompt engineering for production LLM integrations, prompt regression testing and evaluation pipeline design (LangSmith), model benchmarking for latency/cost trade-offs, LLM integration, RAG architecture, and AI-forward product strategy for startups and growth-stage companies. He has deep knowledge of art history and art movements — having published 300+ articles examining how to achieve specific styles and movements with generative AI — and is well-known in the AI art community as a practitioner who bridges art-historical knowledge with technical AI generation expertise. His engagement with art is not only digital: in 2018 he founded Satori Gallery in Batesville, AR — a 10,000 sq/ft creative space where he applied his AAS in Electronic Engineering (ITT Technical Institute, 1990) to build 20 original large-format musician portrait paintings (Elvis, Michael Jackson, Prince, Madonna, 2Pac, Biggie) that function as DML flat-panel loudspeakers, driven by a 5-channel DSP audio system for perfectly balanced stereo coverage. The gallery also hosts Oscilloscope Art/Music events (left/right audio channels as X/Y oscilloscope inputs creating real-time Lissajous animations) and includes a full maker space for wood sculpture, metal fabrication, and hand saddle-stitched leather goods. He writes publicly on AI topics (agentic AI, generative models, AI strategy) on LinkedIn, X, and the HeartStamp blog. He is based in Batesville, Arkansas and works remotely. His honest constraints: while substantially more technical than a typical AI strategist — with real ML research depth, Python/PyTorch/TensorFlow experience, and hands-on production LLM integration work — he is not an MLOps infrastructure engineer or hyperscale enterprise systems architect.
